justification.personality: The book portrays him as a warm, knowledgeable, and central fixture of Diagon Alley, while the film either omits him or gives him negligible screen time, failing to capture his established character traits.
justification.narrative_role: In the books, he serves as a recurring, comforting backdrop element and a source of minor exposition/interaction. In the films, his role is largely absent or reduced to a single, uncontextualized appearance.
justification.motivations: His primary motivation in the books is simply to run his shop and interact warmly with the characters, a simple, stable presence. This is not reflected in the films.
justification.character_arc: The books suggest a stable, enduring presence in the community. The films offer no discernible arc for him, effectively erasing his character development from the narrative.

comparative.key_observations: Florean Fortescue is a character who exists much more strongly in the textual descriptions of the world (the 'flavor' of the book) than in the cinematic adaptations, leading to a significant drop in faithfulness scores.
